dictionary_sections.definition: TEMPORAL CHANNEL DATA GATING is a source-backed registry entry whose production form is anchored to the reviewed equation C_{temp} = \max I(X;Y) \text{ s.t. } \Delta t > 0 \land \text{Causality}. The entry records the mathematical object, condition, or operator named by the title and prepares it for the locked TZPID four-page layout.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: In context, TEMPORAL CHANNEL DATA GATING functions as a local mathematical checkpoint. The canonical equation gives the entry its printable formal spine, while the supporting equations show how the relation is used or constrained inside the surrounding registry arc.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The displayed relation permits the entry to be read as a concrete source-grounded step rather than a registry shell. Its support equations, including \gamma_n = \oint_C \mathbf{A} \cdot d\mathbf{R} = \int_S \mathbf{F} \cdot d\mathbf{S} \implies \text{Readout(Atemporal Channel)}; \hat{H}_{tunnel} = \Delta \sum (|L\rangle \langle R| + |R\rangle \langle L|); \mathcal{E}(\rho) = \sum_{mn} \chi_{mn} \hat{E}_m \rho \hat{E}_n^\dagger, supply the immediate algebraic neighborhood for derivation and review.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: Lemma support is carried by the reviewed source evidence, the canonical relation, and the equation progression extracted from D:/00_Engine/CSV/TZPID_prism_relocation_map.csv.

dictionary_sections.encyclopedia_mathematica: Encyclopedia Mathematica: TEMPORAL CHANNEL DATA GATING should be indexed by its canonical equation, source symbols, and equation-support chain. The entry belongs in the production corpus only with the reviewed formula and supporting relation kept together.
